name: North East Welch
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Wisconsin and Michigan since 1962, offering consumer loans, mortgages, and savings accounts with competitive rates.
description_long:
N.E.W. Credit Union (North East Welch) was established in 1962 and operates as a member-owned financial cooperative serving Brown, Shawano, Oconto, and Marinette counties in Wisconsin, plus Menominee County in Michigan. As a not-for-profit institution, the organization prioritizes member benefit over profit maximization and maintains a strong commitment to community engagement and financial empowerment.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including consumer loans (as low as 4.25% APR), mortgages (as low as 4.99% APR), personal loans (as low as 9.50% APR), and share savings accounts (starting at 0.15% APY). Members have access to digital banking services including mobile deposits, bill pay, fund transfers, and account management through their online platform. The organization also provides financial education resources including interactive demos, videos, and access to financial counseling through an online education center.

N.E.W. Credit Union distinguishes itself through its active community involvement, supporting local organizations and events through sponsorships and volunteer partnerships. The credit union emphasizes member-first service with a stated commitment to treating members "with respect and dignity, and to offer honest, fair deals at all times." Membership benefits include participation in the shared branching network, allowing members to access services at partner credit unions nationwide regardless of relocation.

The institution is well-positioned for consumers within its geographic field of membership who value cooperative banking principles and community-focused finance. However, membership is geographically restricted to specific counties, limiting accessibility for those outside the service area. Interest rates are competitive but not exceptional compared to national online credit unions, and the organization's smaller scale may mean fewer product variations than larger financial institutions.
services: Online account access and digital banking
pros: Membership open to families of eligible members, maintaining access even after moving outside service area
Competitive consumer loan rates starting at 4.25% APR and mortgage rates starting at 4.99% APR
Free financial education center with interactive demos, videos, and personalized financial counseling
Access to shared branching network for nationwide service beyond physical branch locations
Active community support with demonstrated sponsorships and volunteer programs
Member-owned cooperative structure prioritizing fair treatment over profit maximization
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile deposits, bill pay, and account transfers
cons: Geographic membership restrictions limited to specific Wisconsin and Michigan counties
Share savings rates are relatively low at 0.15% APY compared to online savings accounts
Personal loan rates (9.50% APR) are notably higher than consumer loan rates, with limited product differentiation visible
Limited information about additional services like business accounts, credit cards, or investment products
No apparent 24/7 customer service or chat support mentioned on website
best_for: Residents and workers in Brown, Shawano, Oconto, and Marinette counties (WI) or Menominee County (MI) seeking primary banking
Borrowers with local community ties seeking competitive-rate mortgages and auto loans
Members prioritizing financial education and personalized counseling support
Individuals valuing cooperative banking principles and community-focused financial institutions
diagnosis: N.E.W. Credit Union is best for geographically eligible consumers in Wisconsin and Michigan counties who value cooperative banking, community involvement, and personalized service. The main caveat is strict membership eligibility based on county residence or work location—those outside the service area cannot join, making it inaccessible to most consumers despite its favorable terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: North East Welch — North East, PA Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: North East Welch — NCUA-insured credit union in North East, PA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
